relations: Clouds form as a result of the warming of the Earth's surface by the sun
The sun's rays initiate the warming process that leads to cloud formation
Cumulus clouds are a specific type of cloud that can be observed in the sky
Stratus clouds are formed under certain conditions related to cloud formation processes
Cirrus clouds represent another type of cloud that forms in the atmosphere
Water vapor condenses to form clouds when the air reaches its dew point
Air currents play a significant role in the formation and types of clouds
Reaching the dew point is crucial for the condensation process that forms clouds
Cumulus clouds can develop into cumulonimbus clouds, which are associated with more severe weather like thunderstorms
Both cumulus and stratus clouds form through the process of condensation but under different conditions
Cumulus and cirrus clouds represent different stages and conditions of cloud formation
Stratus and cirrus clouds form in different layers of the atmosphere and under different atmospheric conditions
